english: or ghó-keṅ* (gho keng) C., *naṅ-don-čan (nang don can) (or -yod-kan) (yod kan)* W. most learned, very erudite; acc. to Cs. more particularly the mystical sense of religious writings, a higher degree of theology, as it were; naṅ-don-gyi rab-byáms-pa (nang don gyi rab byams pa) a Doctor of Divinity Cs. - naṅ-naṅ-gi (nang nang gi), naṅ-naṅ-nas (nang nang nas) = naṅ-gi (nang gi), naṅ-nas (nang nas) among. - naṅ-pa (nang pa) Buddhist, opp. to pyi-pa (phyi pa), Non-Buddhist, Brahmanist; naṅ-pai lta-ba (nang pai lta ba), bstán-pa (bstan pa), čos (chos), stón-pa (ston pa), čá-lugs (cha lugs), the theory etc. of the Buddhists. - naṅ-po (nang po) an intimate, a bosom-friend Sch. - naṅ-mi (nang mi) members of a household, inmates (ni f) Dom. - naṅ-mig (nang mig) room, apartment, C., W. - *naṅ-yáṅs* (nang yangs) W. wide, spacious, roomy. - naṅ-ról (nang rol) = naṅ-krol (nang khrol) naṅ-ša (nang sha) lining, *naṅ-ša tán-wa* (nang sha tan wa) to cover on the inside, to line, *naṅ-ša-čen* (nang sha chen) C. lined. - naṅ-sél (nang sel) dissension, discrepancy. - naṅ-ysés (nang gses) reciprocal, mutual Wdn. frq.

a sort of potion (thin pap?) consisting of the 'ten impurities', viz. five kinds of flesh (also human flesb), excrements, urine, blood, marrow, and 'byaṅ-séms dkár-po' (byang sems dkar po) (?), all mixed together, transsubstantiated by charms, and changed into bdúd-rtsi (bdud rtsi) or nectar, a small quantity of which is tasted by the devotees, with the Lama at their head. This delicious drink is considered of great importance by the mystics, who seek to obtain spiritual gifts by witchcraft (cf. mdo (mdo) extr.); hence every offering is sprinkled with this potion.

*naṅ-tar* (nang tar) W., C., *naṅ-źiṅ* (nang zhing) C. col. for bźin-du (bzhin du), ltar (ltar), according to, in conformity with, like, as, c. genit. or accus., bka naṅ-tar (bka nang tar), bkai naṅ-tar (bkai nang tar).
resp. for me (me) fire W. (snáṅ-me?) (snang me?).

(ma (ma) Pth.?) the morning; in the morning; naṅ-mo gčig bźin-du (nang mo gcig bzhin du) every morning Pth.; naṅ re (nang re) id.; naṅ re dgoṅs re (nang re dgongs re) every morning and evening; da-náṅ (da nang) this morning; da-náṅ ni gáṅ-nas byon (da nang ni gang nas byon) where do you come from to-day? Mil.; da-náṅ-gi tsó-ba (da nang gi tsho ba) this day's breakfast Mil.; naṅ-nub (nang nub) in the morning and in the evening; naṅ-núb nyi-pyéd ysúm-la (nang nub nyi phyed gsum la) in the morning, in the evening, and at noon. - naṅ-par (nang par) 1. in the morning, naṅ-par sṅa (nang par snga) early in the morning Dzl. 2. the morning esp the tollowing morning, naṅ-par-kyi skál-ba (nang par kyi skal ba) the allowance, the ration for the following morning Glr.
W. (?) naṅ-čuṅ yod (nang chung yod) that is a mere trifle, not worth while, cf. mṅog (mngog).
Cs., *náṅ-la* (nang la) W., the day after to-morrow, B. gnaṅ (gnang).

disease, distemper, malady, sickness, cf. ná-ba (na ba); (the Tibetan science of medicine distinguishes 404 kinds of diseases); mi-nad pyúgs-nad (mi nad phyugs nad) diseases among men and animals Glr.; nad gso-ba (nad gso ba) to cure a disease, nad ₀tsó-ba (nad 'tsho ba), nad sós-par (nad sos par), or źi-bar (zhi bar), or daṅ brál-bar gyúr-ba (dang bral bar gyur ba) to be cured of a disease, to get well, to recover; nád-kyis ₀débs-pa (nad kyis 'debs pa), tébs-pa (thebs pa), to be attacked by a disease, to be taken ill B.; C. more frq.: *né'-kyi gyáb-pa* (ne' kyi gyab pa), *zir-wa* (zir wa), W.: *ná-la nad yoṅ(s)* (na la nad yong(s)); nád-kyi rgyu (nad kyi rgyu), and rkyen (rkyen), v. rkyen (rkyen) 1 and 2. Comp. nad-rkyál (nad rkyal) Wdk. emblem of a deity (meaning not clear). - nád-kaṅ (nad khang) hospital Cs. - nád-go (nad go) seat of a disease Sch. - nád-čan (nad can) ill, sick (little used). - nád-pa (nad pa) 1. a sick person, male or female. 2. adj. ill, sick, séms-čan nád-pa-dag (sems can nad pa dag) S. O. = nád-po (nad po) and nád-bu (nad bu) = nad (nad) Cs., *nád-bu-čan* (nad bu can) W., weak in health, sickly, poorly. - nad-méd (nad med) healthy, hale, in health, (the usual word); nad-méd-par gyúr-čig (nad med par gyur cig) may you recover your health, may you remain in good health, all hail to you! Cs. - nád-med-pa (nad med pa) health, nád-med-pa tób-pa (nad med pa thob pa), rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa) to get well, to recover one's health; nád-med-pa gyúr-ba (nad med pa gyur ba) declining health Thgy. - nad tsúl (nad tshul) the character of a disease S.g. - nad-gźí (nad gzhi) seat, primary cause of a disease(?) Lt. - nad-gyóg (nad g.yog) one attending to sick persons, a nurse; nad-gyóg byéd-pa (nad g.yog byed pa) W. *có-če* (co ce), to nurse.

the act of pressing, urging; pressure, urgency, importunity, kóṅ-rnams-kyi nan ma tégs-par (khong rnams kyi nan ma thegs par) not being able to resist their importunity Mil.; nán-gyis (nan gyis) with urgency,
